title_en: Legacy and Influence on Islamic Intellectual Thought
title_id: Warisan dan Pengaruh terhadap Pemikiran Intelektual Islam
content_id:
Dar' Ta'arudh al-'Aql wan-Naql meninggalkan warisan yang kompleks dan berdampak jauh yang berlangsung selama berabad-abad. Selama kehidupan Ibnu Taimiyyah sendiri, karya ini beredar di kalangan ulama dan menimbulkan kontroversi yang signifikan. Perselisihan-perselisihan publiknya dengan teolog-teolog terkemuka pada zamannya, termasuk para wakil dari kalangan Asy'ariyyah dan lembaga-lembaga Sufi di Kairo dan Damaskus, sering kali berkisar pada tema-tema yang dibahas dalam teks ini. Ia dipenjara berkali-kali sebagian karena posisi-posisinya tentang pertanyaan-pertanyaan ini dipandang mengganggu konsensus teologis yang telah berkembang dalam abad-abad setelah al-Ghazali.

Setelah kematiannya pada tahun 728 H (1328 M), pengaruh Ibnu Taimiyyah dilanjutkan terutama oleh para muridnya, yang paling menonjol adalah Ibnul Qayyim al-Jawziyyah, yang mensistematisasi banyak argumennya dan menulis karya-karya sendiri yang memperluas kritik Ibnu Taimiyyah terhadap teologi filosofis ke domain-domain tambahan. Ibnu Katsir dan al-Dzahabi, keduanya merupakan kontemporer, memiliki rasa hormat yang mendalam kepada Ibnu Taimiyyah bahkan di mana mereka kadang-kadang tidak setuju dengan posisi-posisinya.

Selama beberapa abad, Dar' Ta'arudh dikenal dan dibaca dalam lingkaran-lingkaran ilmiah tetapi tidak mencapai peredaran luas seperti karya-karya yang lebih pendek seperti al-Aqeedah al-Wasithiyyah atau koleksi Fatawa. Bangkitnya gerakan-gerakan reformasi Salafi pada abad kedelapan belas dan kesembilan belas, khususnya gerakan Muhammad ibn 'Abd al-Wahhab di Arabia dan kebangkitan Athari yang lebih luas di seluruh dunia Islam, membawa perhatian yang diperbarui terhadap korpus Ibnu Taimiyyah. Dar' Ta'arudh mulai dipelajari secara lebih sistematis sebagai teks fondasi untuk memahami kritik epistemologisnya terhadap teologi kalam.

Pada abad kedua puluh, penerbitan edisi kritis yang diedit oleh Muhammad Rasyad Salim dalam sebelas jilid membuat teks tersebut sepenuhnya dapat diakses oleh para ulama untuk pertama kalinya dalam bentuk cetak yang andal. Penerbitan ini mengkatalisis keterlibatan akademis yang berkelanjutan dengan karya tersebut. Para ulama filsafat dan teologi Islam — termasuk mereka yang bersimpati kepada tradisi Asy'ariyyah dan Maturidiyyah — mulai terlibat secara serius dengan argumen-argumen Ibnu Taimiyyah, bahkan di mana mereka menolak kesimpulan-kesimpulannya.

Signifikansi abadi teks ini terletak pada beberapa bidang. Ini adalah kritik paling terperinci yang masih ada terhadap metodologi teologi kalam dari dalam tradisi Islam. Ia mengangkat pertanyaan-pertanyaan tentang hubungan antara kerangka-kerangka filosofis yang diwarisi dan penyelidikan rasional yang nyata yang tetap relevan bagi wacana intelektual Islam hari ini. Ia juga menyediakan elaborasi paling lengkap dari pendirian epistemologis Athari: bahwa Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah, ketika dipahami dengan benar, memberikan konten kognitif yang nyata tentang Allah dan alam gaib, dan bahwa konten ini tidak tunduk pada revisi oleh argumen-argumen filosofis yang premis-premisnya sendiri diperdebatkan.

Para ulama dari semua kecenderungan teologis — Athari, Asy'ariyyah, Maturidiyyah, dan mereka yang tidak cocok secara rapi dalam kategori mana pun — telah mengakui Dar' Ta'arudh sebagai salah satu kontribusi paling serius secara intelektual bagi metode teologis Islam. Argumen-argumennya terus dikutip, dilibatkan, dan diperdebatkan dalam keilmuan Islam kontemporer tentang akal, wahyu, dan batas-batas pengetahuan teologis.
content_en:
Dar' Ta'arud al-Aql wan-Naql had a complex and far-reaching legacy that played out over centuries. During Ibn Taymiyyah's own lifetime, the work circulated among scholars and generated significant controversy. His public disputes with leading theologians of his era, including representatives of the Ash'ari and Sufi establishments in Cairo and Damascus, often revolved around the themes treated in this text. He was imprisoned multiple times in part because his positions on these questions were seen as disruptive to the theological consensus that had developed in the centuries after al-Ghazali.

After his death in 728 AH (1328 CE), Ibn Taymiyyah's influence was carried forward primarily by his students, most notably Ibn al-Qayyim al-Jawziyyah, who systematized many of his arguments and wrote his own works extending Ibn Taymiyyah's critique of philosophical theology into additional domains. Ibn Kathir and adh-Dhahabi, both contemporaries, had deep respect for Ibn Taymiyyah even where they occasionally disagreed with his positions.

For several centuries, Dar' Ta'arud was known and read within scholarly circles but did not achieve the broad circulation of shorter works like Al-Aqeedah al-Wasitiyyah or the Fatawa collection. The rise of the Salafi reform movements in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, particularly the movement of Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab in Arabia and the broader Athari revival across the Islamic world, brought renewed attention to Ibn Taymiyyah's corpus. Dar' Ta'arud began to be studied more systematically as the foundational text for understanding his epistemological critique of kalam theology.

In the twentieth century, the publication of a critical edition edited by Muhammad Rashad Salim in eleven volumes made the text fully accessible to scholars for the first time in a reliable printed form. This publication catalyzed sustained academic engagement with the work. Scholars of Islamic philosophy and theology — including those sympathetic to Ash'ari and Maturidi traditions — began to engage seriously with Ibn Taymiyyah's arguments, even where they rejected his conclusions.

The text's lasting significance lies in several areas. It is the most detailed surviving critique of the methodology of kalam theology from within the Islamic tradition. It raises questions about the relationship between inherited philosophical frameworks and genuine rational inquiry that remain relevant to Islamic intellectual discourse today. It also provides the fullest elaboration of the Athari epistemological stance: that the Quran and Sunnah, when correctly understood, provide genuine cognitive content about God and the unseen, and that this content is not subject to revision by philosophical arguments whose premises are themselves contested.

Scholars of all theological persuasions — Athari, Ash'ari, Maturidi, and those who do not fit neatly into any category — have recognized Dar' Ta'arud as one of the most intellectually serious contributions to Islamic theological method. Its arguments continue to be cited, engaged with, and debated in contemporary Islamic scholarship on reason, revelation, and the boundaries of theological knowledge.
content_ar: Dar' Ta'arud al-Aql wan-Naql had a complex and far-reaching legacy that played out over centuries. خلال ابن تيمية's own lifetime, the work circulated among العلماء and generated المهم controversy. His public disputes with leading theologians of his era, بما في ذلك representatives of the الأشعري and Sufi establishments in Cairo and Damascus, often revolved around the themes treated in this text. He was imprisoned multiple times in part لأن his positions on these questions were seen as disruptive to the theological consensus that had developed in the centuries بعد al-Ghazali. بعد his الموت in 728 AH (1328 CE), ابن تيمية's influence was carried forward primarily by his students, most notably ابن القيم al-Jawziyyah, who systematized many of his arguments and wrote his own works extending ابن تيمية's critique of philosophical theology into additional domains. Ibn Kathir and adh-Dhahabi, both contemporaries, had deep الاحترام for ابن تيمية even where they occasionally disagreed with his positions. For several centuries, Dar' Ta'arud was known and read within العلمي circles but did not achieve the broad circulation of shorter works like Al-العقيدة al-Wasitiyyah or the الفتاوى collection. The rise of the السلفي reform movements in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, particularly the movement of محمد ﷺ ibn Abd al-Wahhab in Arabia and the broader الأثري revival across the Islamic الدنيا, brought renewed attention to ابن تيمية's corpus. Dar' Ta'arud began to be studied more systematically as the foundational text for الفهم his epistemological critique of علم الكلام theology. In the twentieth century, the publication of a البالغ الأهمية edition edited by محمد ﷺ Rashad Salim in eleven volumes made the text fully accessible to العلماء for the أولاً time in a reliable printed form. This publication catalyzed sustained الأكاديمي engagement with the work. العلماء of Islamic philosophy and theology — بما في ذلك those sympathetic to الأشعري and الماتريدي traditions — began to engage seriously with ابن تيمية's arguments, even where they rejected his conclusions. The text's lasting significance lies in several areas. It is the most detailed surviving critique of the methodology of علم الكلام theology from within the Islamic التراث. It raises questions about the relationship between inherited philosophical frameworks and genuine rational inquiry that remain relevant to Islamic intellectual discourse today. It أيضاً provides the fullest elaboration of the الأثري epistemological stance: that the القرآن الكريم and السنة, عندما correctly understood, provide genuine cognitive content about God and the unseen, and that this content is not subject to revision by philosophical arguments whose premises are themselves contested. العلماء of all theological persuasions — الأثري, الأشعري, الماتريدي, and those who do not fit neatly into any category — have recognized Dar' Ta'arud as one of the most intellectually serious contributions to Islamic theological method. Its arguments continue to be cited, engaged with, and debated in contemporary Islamic scholarship on العقل, الوحي, and the boundaries of theological العلم.
